rawOutput:
{
  "topic": "Psoriasis Pathophysiology: A 10-Mark Answer",
  "topic_slug": "psoriasis-pathophysiology-10-mark-pg-exam",
  "level": "Advanced (Post-Graduate Medical)",
  "goal": "Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)",
  "estimated_total_minutes": 300,
  "modules": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "title": "Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers",
      "estimated_minutes": 45,
      "description": "Lays the foundation by exploring the specific genetic susceptibility loci (PSORS1-9) and how environmental triggers prime the immune system, essential for the opening sections of a PG-level theory answer.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Identify and explain the role of PSORS loci, HLA-Cw6, and specific environmental triggers in initiating psoriasis.",
        "concepts": [
          "PSORS1-9 genetic loci",
          "HLA-Cw6 allele association",
          "IL23R and TNFAIP3 gene mutations",
          "Koebner phenomenon mechanism",
          "Streptococcal pharyngitis (guttate psoriasis trigger)",
          "Drug-induced triggers (Lithium, Beta-blockers, NSAIDs)"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31 Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": []
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "application"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "title": "The Spark: Innate Immunity and the Initiation Phase",
      "estimated_minutes": 50,
      "description": "Dives into the immediate molecular response to keratinocyte stress. This module builds the first half of your pathophysiological flowchart.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Map the cascade from keratinocyte injury to the activation of Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells via antimicrobial peptides.",
        "concepts": [
          "Keratinocyte stress and self-DNA/RNA release",
          "Role of LL-37 (Cathelicidin)",
          "LL-37/DNA complex formation",
          "Toll-like receptors (TLR7/9)",
          "Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells (pDCs) activation",
          "Interferon-alpha (IFN-\u03b1) surge"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31 Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 5,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "pathway_sequencing"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 3,
      "title": "The Engine: Adaptive Immunity and the IL-23/Th17 Axis",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "The highest-yield module for your exam. This covers the 'master regulator' pathways that examiners look for, detailing the shift from innate to adaptive immunity.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Diagram the IL-23/Th17 axis and differentiate the specific effector functions of IL-17A, IL-17F, and IL-22.",
        "concepts": [
          "Myeloid Dendritic Cells (mDCs) migration and activation",
          "IL-12 pathway and Th1 cell differentiation (TNF-\u03b1, IFN-\u03b3)",
          "IL-23 as the master regulator",
          "Th17 and Th22 cell survival and proliferation",
          "Effector mechanisms of IL-17A and IL-17F",
          "Effector mechanisms of IL-22"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31 Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "The Spark: Innate Immunity and the Initiation Phase"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 6,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "conceptual",
          "molecular_interaction",
          "true_false"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 4,
      "title": "Clinical Translation: Histopathology and the Feed-Forward Loop",
      "estimated_minutes": 60,
      "description": "Connects the invisible molecular immunology to the visible histological slides and clinical signs you see on the ward. Crucial for scoring marks on clinico-pathological correlation.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Correlate specific cytokine activity with epidermal changes, histological hallmarks, and clinical signs (Auspitz sign).",
        "concepts": [
          "Keratinocyte hyperproliferation (28 days to 3-5 days transit time)",
          "The inflammatory feed-forward circuit (CXCL8, CXCL1)",
          "Acanthosis and elongated rete ridges",
          "Parakeratosis pathophysiology",
          "Munro\u2019s Microabscesses (neutrophil infiltration)",
          "Vascular dilation, angiogenesis, and the Auspitz sign"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31 Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "The Engine: Adaptive Immunity and the IL-23/Th17 Axis"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 6,
        "pass_threshold": 80,
        "question_types": [
          "clinical_correlation",
          "conceptual",
          "image_feature_association"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "id": 5,
      "title": "Exam Synthesis: The 10-Mark Architecture & Flowcharts",
      "estimated_minutes": 85,
      "description": "Brings the entire 4-month mastery into a highly replicable, structured framework. Focuses on spatial memory, drawing flowcharts, and hitting the examiner's grading rubric.",
      "plan": {
        "goal": "Construct a complete, timed 10-mark theory answer including a comprehensive immunopathogenesis flowchart and biological target correlations.",
        "concepts": [
          "Structuring the 10-mark answer (Intro, Etiology, Innate, Adaptive, Histology)",
          "Designing the macro-flowchart (Trigger -> AMPs -> pDCs -> mDCs -> IL-23 -> Th17 -> Keratinocytes)",
          "Integrating the 'Feed-Forward' concept clearly in diagrams",
          "Bonus point strategy: Linking pathophysiology to biologic targets (Anti-TNF-\u03b1, Anti-IL-23, Anti-IL-17)",
          "Summary tables for rapid examiner review"
        ],
        "sourceRefs": [
          "Chapter 31 Psoriasis"
        ],
        "prerequisites": [
          "Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers",
          "Clinical Translation: Histopathology and the Feed-Forward Loop"
        ]
      },
      "subtopics": [],
      "generated": false,
      "checkpoint": {
        "type": "quiz",
        "num_questions": 3,
        "pass_threshold": 100,
        "question_types": [
          "essay_structuring",
          "diagram_reproduction",
          "rubric_checklist"
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}

parsed.goal: Post-graduate dermatology exam preparation (specifically structured for a 10-mark theory answer)

modules.title: Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers
modules.description: Lays the foundation by exploring the specific genetic susceptibility loci (PSORS1-9) and how environmental triggers prime the immune system, essential for the opening sections of a PG-level theory answer.

plan.goal: Identify and explain the role of PSORS loci, HLA-Cw6, and specific environmental triggers in initiating psoriasis.

modules.title: The Spark: Innate Immunity and the Initiation Phase
modules.description: Dives into the immediate molecular response to keratinocyte stress. This module builds the first half of your pathophysiological flowchart.

plan.goal: Map the cascade from keratinocyte injury to the activation of Plasmacytoid Dendritic Cells via antimicrobial peptides.
prerequisites: Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers

modules.title: The Engine: Adaptive Immunity and the IL-23/Th17 Axis
modules.description: The highest-yield module for your exam. This covers the 'master regulator' pathways that examiners look for, detailing the shift from innate to adaptive immunity.

plan.goal: Diagram the IL-23/Th17 axis and differentiate the specific effector functions of IL-17A, IL-17F, and IL-22.
concepts: Myeloid Dendritic Cells (mDCs) migration and activation
IL-12 pathway and Th1 cell differentiation (TNF-α, IFN-γ)
Th17 and Th22 cell survival and proliferation
Effector mechanisms of IL-17A and IL-17F
prerequisites: The Spark: Innate Immunity and the Initiation Phase

modules.title: Clinical Translation: Histopathology and the Feed-Forward Loop
modules.description: Connects the invisible molecular immunology to the visible histological slides and clinical signs you see on the ward. Crucial for scoring marks on clinico-pathological correlation.

plan.goal: Correlate specific cytokine activity with epidermal changes, histological hallmarks, and clinical signs (Auspitz sign).
concepts: Keratinocyte hyperproliferation (28 days to 3-5 days transit time)
Vascular dilation, angiogenesis, and the Auspitz sign
prerequisites: The Engine: Adaptive Immunity and the IL-23/Th17 Axis

modules.title: Exam Synthesis: The 10-Mark Architecture & Flowcharts
modules.description: Brings the entire 4-month mastery into a highly replicable, structured framework. Focuses on spatial memory, drawing flowcharts, and hitting the examiner's grading rubric.

plan.goal: Construct a complete, timed 10-mark theory answer including a comprehensive immunopathogenesis flowchart and biological target correlations.
concepts: Structuring the 10-mark answer (Intro, Etiology, Innate, Adaptive, Histology)
Designing the macro-flowchart (Trigger -> AMPs -> pDCs -> mDCs -> IL-23 -> Th17 -> Keratinocytes)
Integrating the 'Feed-Forward' concept clearly in diagrams
Bonus point strategy: Linking pathophysiology to biologic targets (Anti-TNF-α, Anti-IL-23, Anti-IL-17)
Summary tables for rapid examiner review
prerequisites: Etiology: Genetic Loci and Environmental Triggers
Clinical Translation: Histopathology and the Feed-Forward Loop
